meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop building in the dark. Learn to implement production-grade analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to escape tutorial hell and validate your SaaS.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell thrives on lack of feedback loops. By integrating event-based analytics into your MVP, you force data-driven decision-making. Using Antigravity to instrument your core user flows and Claude Code to automate the integration, you bridge the gap between 'coding a project' and 'shipping a profitable SaaS business'.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching tutorials, cloning repositories, and tweaking CSS, yet your dashboard remains empty. The silent killer of every founder is not a lack of code, but the lack of objective reality. You are trapped in a loop of endless building, disconnected from the signals that actually drive revenue.

Real founders don't guess; they measure. To escape the gravity of tutorial hell, you must stop treating your app as a collection of features and start treating it as a data-generating machine. We are going to wire your MVP for insight using high-leverage tooling, ensuring every line of code you ship provides actionable intelligence on your user's journey.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before instrumenting, identify the 3 events that signify value realization. This prevents 'data hoarding' and focuses your build on conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my existing codebase and identify the core conversion funnel. Propose an event schema consisting of exactly 3 events that track user activation and value realization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify the top 3 user actions that correlate with long-term retention in this SaaS boilerplate.'

steps.title: Automated Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to inject analytics hooks into your event-driven architecture without manual boilerplate bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable event-tracking decorator for my primary API routes. Ensure it captures userId, timestamp, and event metadata asynchronously.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Implement the analytics decorator on all POST routes in /api/v1/'

steps.title: Integrate Production Telemetry
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a robust analytics provider. We avoid client-side clutter by routing events through your backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a modular analytics provider interface. I want to swap providers (e.g., Mixpanel vs PostHog) via environment variables without changing business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an analytics service provider pattern and integrate it into the app config.'

steps.title: Validation of Data Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure your events are flowing from development to production. A broken analytics pipeline is worse than no analytics at all.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that simulates user journeys and asserts that the analytics events are triggered and formatted correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Verify event payload delivery for the signup flow integration test.'

steps.title: Automating Insight Reporting
steps.description: Close the feedback loop by having Claude generate a weekly summary of your event data, turning raw logs into founder-level strategy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that aggregates the last 7 days of event logs and generates a summary of the conversion rate between 'signup' and 'value_realization'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cron job that logs the weekly conversion summary to the console.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you can't describe what decision you'll make based on a data point, don't track it.
Use server-side tracking to bypass ad-blockers, which can kill up to 30% of your client-side analytics data.
Implement an 'event registry' file to act as a single source of truth for all event names and schemas across your team.
Track 'failed' events as aggressively as 'success' events; failures often reveal where your UX is leaking revenue.
Anonymize PII at the edge before it hits your analytics provider to maintain GDPR/CCPA compliance by default.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, set up a dashboard that you check every morning before writing new code. If the numbers aren't moving, stop building features and start optimizing the existing flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility required to turn your MVP into a scalable, data-backed business. Stop guessing and start shipping with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
